Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Dongguang Photoelectric / DG Display.

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Dongguang Photoelectric (Nanjing) Co., Ltd. presents the tragic profile of a severely contracted, once-massive foreign-invested LCD manufacturing plant. Registered as a joint venture (Hong Kong/Macao/Taiwan investment) with a staggering paid-in capital of 373.8 million RMB, it was clearly built as a major Tier-1 component supplier in the display panel supply chain. The corporate email domain '@didsp.co.kr' strongly links it to a Korean corporate parent (likely Dongguang Display). However, its 2024 workforce collapsed to merely 7 insured employees. Manufacturing LCD modules and backlight units requires immense cleanrooms and thousands of workers. Therefore, this massive facility in the Nanjing Economic and Technological Development Zone has undeniably ceased primary manufacturing. It currently exists as a 'zombie factory' or an asset-holding shell, retaining a skeleton crew of 7 people likely to manage legacy assets, maintain factory grounds, or handle the residual legal and financial affairs of its foreign parent company.

**Risk Profile Summary:**
Trust Tier: **L1_REGISTERED** (Score: 72)
Operational Status: DORMANT FACTORY / ASSET HOLDING NODE (EXTREME CONTRACTION)
